answers.where: Yellow Backrooms corridor facing a non-Euclidean room aperture with material-kind-aware native geometry, stronger center light spill, side shadow falloff, floor grout, wall seams, back-wall strips, ceiling grid, jambs, threshold, fixture, conduit, and landmark column
answers.action: Use the copied mark as evidence, find the missing handle, and test the locked door
answers.response: The mark appears inside the impossible room, and the runtime now shades 432 native nt_gfx overlay vertices with material-kind lighting and shadow response
answers.reward: The player learns the space can be tested and later stabilized by fitting the found handle
answers.game_look: Native 3D liminal horror view with deeper portal room, stronger solid-room lighting, shadowed aperture, stained wallpaper, carpet seams, floor/wall/ceiling detail, minimal journal UI, and texture-backed material geometry

issues.text: Material-kind lighting improves solidity and shadow response, but the room is still an overlay pass rather than a fully integrated physically lit 3D space
The non-Euclidean hook is clearer and scarier, but the screenshot still falls short of the distinctive high-quality horror target needed to stand out among many Backrooms games

not_covered.reason: covered by earlier first-screen gates, not the focus of T0010 portal proof
covered by blackout/sprint tasks, not this portal proof

problem: The portal proof now reads more solid because material kinds drive different alpha, light spill, side shadows, seam darkening, and depth falloff, but it still lacks true integrated 3D surfaces and render-target-backed lighting
next: Move from overlay-lit material detail to proper authored 3D room surfaces or T0011 render-target portal rendering, then revisit product gate for art_quality and audience_fit
